agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Higher log fundraising efficiency (contributions raised per dollar spent) naturally correlates with a larger volume of total contributions.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Thornton (2006) discusses return to scale efficiencies in fundraising.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A highly statistically significant positive correlation was found. Nonprofits with higher log fundraising efficiency raise substantially more contributions on average, confirming that efficiency scales alongside donor resource mobilization capacity.
